rule_text: Every proposed rule must identify which named platform values from policyos_platform_values_v1.md it advances, protects, constrains, or places into tension.
purpose: Make the values layer an explicit design input rather than an implied background assumption.

rule_text: No proposed rule may be accepted if it undermines human dignity, equal standing, real liberty, meaningful accountability, transparency, practical access, or enforceable fairness without an explicit, compelling, and reviewable justification.
purpose: Prevent structurally unjust rules from passing procedural checks.

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be reviewed for whether it creates unequal classes of people, selective burdens, arbitrary exclusions, or second-class access in practice.
purpose: Force equality and fairness review into authoring, not just later critique.

rule_text: Every proposed rule involving enforcement, punishment, or state power must be reviewed for whether it preserves accountability, proportionality, due process, and meaningful review.
purpose: Keep coercive power aligned with the platform’s core constraints.
applies_to: Rules involving coercion, punishment, denial, or state force

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be reviewed for whether it creates capture opportunities, unreviewable discretion, hidden power, or conditions under which any actor becomes functionally above the law.
purpose: Make anti-capture and anti-impunity part of rule design itself.

rule_text: Every proposed rule affecting basic needs, essential services, or rights access must be reviewed for whether it preserves practical usability, not merely formal entitlement.
purpose: Tie drafting discipline to real-world freedom and material access.
applies_to: Rules affecting rights, benefits, services, and basic needs

rule_text: Every proposed rule with environmental, infrastructure, industrial, extractive, land-use, agricultural, health, or technological impact must be reviewed for whether it degrades ecological habitability, clean air, clean water, safe food systems, or the shared environmental conditions required for freedom and health.
purpose: Make environmental habitability a first-class normative review gate.
applies_to: Rules with environmental or system-level public-health impact

rule_text: Where core platform values come into tension within a proposed rule, the conflict must be surfaced explicitly, named against the values document, and resolved in the drafting rather than hidden inside vague language or implementation discretion.
purpose: Force value conflicts into the open.

rule_text: Where a rule uses general standards, broad protective language, or open-textured terms to preserve future adaptability, that flexibility must be anchored to explicit baseline protections and stated purpose and may not be interpreted to dilute dignity, equality, liberty, transparency, accountability, enforceability, ecological protection, or the rule’s core protective intent.
purpose: Preserve future flexibility without letting vagueness undermine the law.
applies_to: All rules using broad or adaptive language

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must identify the actor being regulated, the conduct being required or prohibited, the trigger condition, and the intended outcome.
purpose: Force rules to specify who does what, when, and why.

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must specify the enforcement authority, enforcement mechanism, remedy or penalty, and review or appeal path where violations or denials are possible.
purpose: Prevent symbolic rules with no execution path.

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must be written in language that is clear enough to implement, review, and audit without relying on unstated assumptions.
purpose: Reduce ambiguity and hidden discretion.

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must distinguish between the normative requirement, the implementation mechanism, and any optional examples or commentary.
purpose: Prevent mixing principle, mechanism, and prose into one undifferentiated rule.

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must identify whether it creates a right, duty, prohibition, process requirement, design constraint, reporting obligation, funding obligation, or enforcement mechanism.
purpose: Force consistent rule typing.

rule_text: Every proposed policy rule must identify the level at which it operates: constitutional, statutory, regulatory, administrative, institutional, contractual, or technical-system design.
purpose: Prevent category errors in implementation planning.

rule_text: Every policy section must separate universal principle statements from operational rules and from explanatory prose.
purpose: Keep documents structurally legible.

rule_text: Where a policy requires discretion, the rule set must state the governing standards, documentation duties, and review mechanisms that constrain that discretion.
purpose: Prevent unbounded discretionary power.

rule_text: Rules must balance explicit baseline commitments with bounded interpretive flexibility: the baseline must be specific enough to establish enforceable minimums and clear intent, while any open-textured language must not be interpretable in ways that defeat the rule’s purpose or protections.
purpose: Preserve both enforceability and future adaptability without letting vagueness hollow out the rule.

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be tested for loopholes, exploit paths, burden-shifting, selective enforcement risk, and foreseeable abuse patterns before acceptance.
purpose: Make adversarial review part of rule design.

rule_text: Every proposed rule affecting rights, access, or eligibility must be tested against practical barriers including cost, distance, disability, language, timing, digital access, and paperwork burden.
purpose: Ensure formal rights become usable rights.

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be checked for conflict with existing rules in the same pillar, overlapping pillars, and applicable system overlays.
purpose: Preserve internal consistency.

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be reviewed for whether it creates perverse incentives, hidden rationing, delay incentives, denial incentives, or extraction incentives.
purpose: Prevent structurally bad outcomes.

rule_text: Every high-risk rule must be evaluated against bad-actor behavior, edge cases, noncompliance patterns, and likely institutional workarounds.
purpose: Stress-test the rule before adoption.

rule_text: Every rights-limiting or coercive rule must identify the least-abusive alternative considered and explain why the chosen design is justified.
purpose: Force necessity and proportionality review.
applies_to: Rules that restrict rights or use coercive state power

rule_text: Every proposed rule set must define what successful implementation, implementation failure, non-enforcement, and systemic breakdown would look like in practice.
purpose: Make future evaluation possible.

rule_text: No rule should be accepted as complete unless it defines who enforces it, how enforcement begins, what evidence is needed, and what happens when enforcement fails.
purpose: Convert policy from aspiration into executable design.

rule_text: Where violations are likely to recur or be concealed, the rule set must specify monitoring, audit, reporting, inspection, or detection mechanisms appropriate to the risk.
purpose: Require detection, not just punishment.
applies_to: Rules in fraud-prone, opaque, or high-risk areas

rule_text: Where the platform proposes penalties, it must specify escalation logic and the threshold for moving from ordinary enforcement to structural intervention or individual liability.
purpose: Force proportional but real consequences.

rule_text: Where a rule depends on reporting, the rule set must specify reporting format, cadence, auditability, and consequences for false, missing, or manipulated reporting.
purpose: Prevent fake compliance through bad reporting design.

rule_text: Where a rule creates appeal, challenge, or review rights, the rule set must identify timelines, access standards, record access, and decision authority for that review.
purpose: Make review rights operational rather than symbolic.

rule_text: Every proposed rule must be placed at the narrowest scope that still preserves consistency: kernel, overlay, pillar, cross-pillar coordination rule, foundation prose, or strategy prose.
purpose: Prevent over-centralization of rule layers.
applies_to: All new policy and system rules

rule_text: A rule belongs in the universal system layer only if it applies across multiple pillars as a design invariant rather than as a domain-specific policy preference.
purpose: Protect the kernel from scope drift.

rule_text: A rule belongs in an overlay when it is cross-domain but conditional, meaning it applies only to pillars with a recurring design problem such as geography, federalism, regulation, enforcement architecture, or AI.
purpose: Keep overlays distinct from the kernel.

rule_text: A rule belongs in a pillar when its implementation details, metrics, institutions, or harms are domain-specific enough that universal treatment would reduce clarity or accuracy.
purpose: Keep specifics where they belong.

rule_text: A statement belongs in prose rather than canon when it expresses values, narrative framing, project identity, strategy, or history without creating a design invariant or enforceable policy requirement.
purpose: Prevent canon from absorbing rhetoric.

rule_text: Every major policy section must identify which rules are core, which are implementation-dependent, and which are expected to require future revision as conditions change.
purpose: Make maintenance explicit.

rule_text: When a rule is revised, the revision must state whether the change closes a loophole, updates a condition, resolves a conflict, improves enforceability, or narrows overbreadth.
purpose: Preserve rationale for future maintenance.

rule_text: Deprecated rules should be superseded with visible traceability rather than silently removed when provenance or interpretation history matters.
purpose: Preserve design history and prevent accidental reintroduction of bad rules.

rule_text: Every pillar must periodically review for missing policy areas, stale assumptions, under-enforced rules, and newly visible system vulnerabilities.
purpose: Make gap analysis recurring, not one-off.

rule_text: Where a system overlay applies to a pillar, the pillar should explicitly state how that overlay is implemented locally rather than assuming the inheritance is obvious.
purpose: Make overlay inheritance concrete.
